text: Xingyu Lai is an interdisciplinary artist from China. Her art practice is deeply rooted in painting, which serves as the foundation of her work, but has organically expanded into sculpture, ceramics, photography, and interactive installations over time. She focuses on the subtle habits of the body, exploring how innate actions—breathing, nursing, and circulating blood—connect individuals to one another and to the systems they inhabit. These small, often overlooked behaviors shape perception, emotion, and psychology in profound ways. Centered on the human experience, her work investigates the relationships between the body, identity, and everyday life. Her practice aims to reveal these invisible links, allowing viewers to sense the presence and significance of the body through observation or participation.
